summary: The notebook implements a spatio-temporal trajectory prediction pipeline for the NFL Big Data Bowl 2026 competition. It combines a deterministic geometric baseline with a custom PyTorch STTransformer model that learns residual corrections for player movement. The pipeline extracts approximately 167 features including geometric endpoints, route clustering, and weighted neighbor embeddings, processes them into 10-frame sequences, and trains 10 fold-specific models using GroupKFold. It ensembles predictions from this transformer-based model and a separate GRU model, applying field boundary clipping and direction inversion before generating the final submission.
competition_flow: Loads NFL player tracking CSVs, computes geometric, route, and neighbor features, trains 10 fold-specific STTransformer models with GroupKFold, and ensembles their predictions with a GRU model to output clipped 2D trajectories.
data_reading: Reads train/input_2023_w*.csv and train/output_2023_w*.csv via pd.read_csv and pd.concat
API receives Polars DataFrames converted to Pandas for processing
data_processing: Scales features with StandardScaler per fold
Creates 10-frame sliding windows with padding for short sequences
Fills NaNs with group means or zeros
Applies time-decay weighting during loss computation
features_engineering: 13 geometric endpoint features (targeted receiver convergence, defender mirroring, required velocity/acceleration, alignment)
Route clustering via KMeans on trajectory metrics (straightness, turns, depth, width, speed)
Weighted GNN-lite neighbor embeddings (dx, dy, dvx, dvy for allies/opponents within radius 30, top 6 neighbors)
Opponent interaction features (nearest distance, closing speed, mirror WR tracking)
Temporal lags (1-5), rolling stats (3, 5), EMAs, and play progress/time features
ensembling: Averages predictions from the STTransformer/GNN model and a GRU model, then clips coordinates to field boundaries and inverts direction based on play direction.
insights: Combining a deterministic geometric baseline with learned neural corrections improves trajectory prediction accuracy.
Route clustering and spatial neighbor embeddings effectively capture player roles and interactions.
Time-decayed loss weighting prioritizes earlier prediction steps in trajectory forecasting.
